content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Found 2 issues: accepted planned-elsewhere mappings can be lost if the prompt is re-read from mutable disk state after final review, and the milestone-state loader still returns superseded mappings when the latest completion round has zero planned-elsewhere entries.

## Amendments

1. Use the already-validated final-review result (or an immutable prompt snapshot/hash from the run) when persisting/reconstructing planned-elsewhere mappings instead of re-reading the mutable project prompt from disk. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:644-656` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1526-1536` already strip invalid `mapped_to_bead_id` values before acceptance, but `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7614-7642` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:968-1015` fail closed if that later prompt read is empty or changed. A transient read error, prompt rewrite, or prompt-reference change after final review can therefore cause accepted planned-elsewhere findings to be silently dropped instead of being journaled and sidecar-persisted.
   Rationale: This violates the durability/audit goal of the bead: once a mapping has been accepted and the aggregate committed, later prompt-file drift should not be able to erase it.
2. Fix the milestone-state loader so later completion rounds that contain zero planned-elsewhere mappings can still supersede earlier rounds. Right now `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2516-2535` computes the max round only from mappings that exist, so a round-1 mapping survives forever if round 2 resolved the concern and emitted no mapping. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:741-759` has to paper over this by consulting final-review aggregates, which means the public `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings` API and the `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` projection can still report stale state to any other caller.
   Rationale: The service claims to rebuild authoritative milestone state, but it currently cannot represent the common case 'latest round has no planned-elsewhere findings'. That leaves a leaky abstraction and stale data outside the one reconciler call site that knows about the workaround.
